root_cause: The class-coverage-expansion-probe run (run-0003-0001) achieved val ROC-AUC 0.6652, essentially flat versus the prior baseline (0.6651). The lane leader MLP probe from run-0002-0003 holds 0.6801, which is the best score in branch. The primary bottleneck is the 0.327 train-val gap (0.992 train vs 0.665 val), indicating severe overfitting or domain shift. Padded_cmap at 0.062 confirms weak segment-level localization. Class coverage expansion alone produced no material gain. Five rounds of micro-tuning have been exhausted per the axis-exhaustion negative prior. Before packaging a submission, the higher-value move is to close the gap between the current best (0.6652) and the lane leader (0.6801) by adopting the MLP probe head configuration and then pursuing calibration and domain-shift mitigation.
why: The research verdict says submission-required, but submitting at 0.6652 when the lane leader holds 0.6801 leaves 0.015 AUC on the table. The MLP probe head from run-0002-0003 is a positive prior at 0.68 confidence and has already demonstrated the best score. The negative prior vetoes further micro-tuning on the current head architecture, but does not veto adopting the proven MLP configuration as a baseline. A submission at 0.6801 is materially stronger than one at 0.6652. We should plan a multi-branch follow-up: (1) adopt the MLP probe head as the primary submission candidate, (2) add calibration (temperature scaling) as a parallel branch, and (3) explore domain-shift augmentation as a speculative branch. This preserves branch-search space per the decision rules rather than collapsing to a single calibration tweak.
next_title: MLP probe head adoption + calibration + domain-shift mitigation
portfolio_intent: Close the 0.015 gap between current (0.6652) and lane leader (0.6801) by adopting the proven MLP probe configuration, then calibrate and submit. Open speculative domain-shift branch for upside.

branch_mix.title: MLP probe head adoption from run-0002-0003
branch_mix.hypothesis: Adopting the lane-leader MLP probe head (0.6801) as the primary pipeline configuration will reproduce or exceed that score in the current branch context
branch_mix.rationale: Strongest positive prior; proven score; no architectural risk

branch_mix.title: Temperature scaling calibration on MLP logits
branch_mix.hypothesis: Temperature scaling on the MLP probe head logits will improve ROC-AUC ranking quality by correcting the miscalibrated prior fusion weights
branch_mix.rationale: Low-risk post-hoc calibration; prior fusion barely exceeds raw val (0.662 vs 0.665)

branch_mix.title: Domain-shift augmentation on training soundscapes
branch_mix.hypothesis: Frequency-domain augmentation (specAugment, noise injection) on training soundscapes will narrow the 0.327 train-val gap and improve generalization
branch_mix.rationale: High ceiling but uncertain; train-val gap is the fundamental blocker

problem_frame.focus: MLP probe head adoption and calibration for submission parity
